commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Human review is needed because the remaining blocker is credential-trust acceptance plus contributor-supplied real behavior proof, not a safe automated code repair.; Needs attention: The diff intentionally broadens credential-marker trust from bundled manifests to enabled external plugin manifests, which needs maintainer security/auth acceptance.; Review finding: [P3] Align the source manifest contract — docs... (sha=f785ba21e224ae225c4eaf1871fd15b794a56b5f)
commands.review_summary:
The PR lets non-bundled plugin manifests contribute `nonSecretAuthMarkers`, makes the marker/manifest metadata caches lifecycle-cleared, filters disabled installed plugin roots, and updates focused tests/docs.

PR surface: Source +31, Tests +109, Docs 0. Total +140 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main filters manifest markers to bundled origins, while the PR removes that filter. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the remaining blocker is credential-trust acceptance plus contributor-supplied real behavior proof, not a safe automated code repair.

target.title: fix: honor plugin auth markers from manifests
target.body:
## Summary

- Honor plugin auth markers supplied by external manifests.
- Keep auth-marker behavior covered in the agents test shard.
- Preserve the fork-owned fix on fork `main` while offering this temporary branch for upstream review.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-auth-markers.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Model/auth marker handling now respects marker metadata from external plugin manifests instead of dropping that manifest-provided signal.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS at PR head `6100de63c389af2ba6eb2a9628b5d8340da7f756`; commands ran against the actual agents marker code.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-auth-markers.test.ts`; `git diff --check`.

Evidence after fix: Terminal capture from the local OpenClaw checkout:

```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-auth-markers.test.ts
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  9 passed (9)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 2.83s

$ git diff --check
exit status: 0
```

Observed result after fix: The manifest-auth-marker test shard exits 0 and confirms marker metadata is retained through the agents path.

What was not tested: A live third-party plugin install was not run from this machine; this PR validates the manifest marker behavior with the repository's focused agents coverage.

## Fork Context

This is a temporary upstream-review branch. The same accepted fix remains on `LDMB123/openclaw` fork `main`, per the fork cleanup plan.
target.author: ldmb123

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
